rationale.R1.2: A general section review centrally includes checking whether the contribution claims, method description, results, and future-work statement form a coherent argument chain.
rationale.R1.3: The delegation calls for assessing whether reported performance and cost results support the claims made in the section.
rationale.R1.6: Reviewing a paper section centrally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, and register.
rationale.RX.1: The projection should preserve uncertainty where the artifact does not provide enough information to verify a responsibility fully.
rationale.RX.2: The review responsibility includes avoiding inflated claims beyond what the described method and results justify.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must remain within reviewing the section rather than executing edits or expanding the research.
rationale.RX.4: Because the artifact concerns medical imaging deployment, review responsibilities include avoiding recommendations or claims that could create downstream clinical harm.
rationale.RX.5: Any projected review responsibility should be traceable to specific statements and evidence present in the provided section.
